jokes.text: Hey, buddy, do we have to be kneeling this entire time? Yeah, this is not comfortable.
jokes.setup: Mac has forced everyone to kneel in darkness for his ceremonial Mac Day beginning
jokes.punchline: Immediate complaints about the uncomfortable position undermining Mac's grand ceremony
jokes.explanation: Establishes the group's dynamic perfectly - Mac's grandiose plans immediately met with practical complaints. Great economy and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Hey, buddy, do we have to be kneeling this entire time?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, this is not comfortable.

jokes.text: If anyone is caught complaining? An extra day is added to Mac Day. If you cannot resist the urge to complain, you are allowed one scream a day to the ceremonial screaming pillow.
jokes.setup: Mac has elaborate rules for his special day
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific rule about a 'ceremonial screaming pillow' as the only approved complaint outlet
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Mac logic - creating an elaborate system even for complaints. The 'ceremonial screaming pillow' is genuinely funny and quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: If anyone is caught complaining? An extra day is added to Mac Day. If you cannot resist the urge to complain, you are allowed one scream a day to the ceremonial screaming pillow.

jokes.text: Charlie, when it was your day, did I complain about searching for goblins for ten hours straight?
jokes.setup: Mac defending his day by referencing Charlie's previous day
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of searching for 'goblins' as if this is normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie characterization - of course his day involved hunting mythical creatures. Good callback structure.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie, when it was your day, did I complain about searching for goblins for ten hours straight?

jokes.text: Well, ghouls, and, no, they didn't complain, 'cause we found three. It was pretty sweet. We found none. Yeah? We found no ghouls, we found no goblins, we found no gremlins, because they don't exist. Well...
jokes.setup: Charlie defending his day where they searched for supernatural creatures
jokes.punchline: Charlie's delusional insistence they found three ghouls, immediately contradicted by Dennis, followed by Charlie's uncertain 'Well...'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie character moment - his delusional confidence immediately deflated. The escalating list and his hesitant 'Well...' at the end is great structure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, ghouls, and, no, they didn't complain, 'cause we found three. It was pretty sweet.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We found none. Yeah? We found no ghouls, we found no goblins, we found no gremlins, because they don't exist.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well...

jokes.text: On the first day, God said, 'Let there be... Mac.'
jokes.setup: Mac is recreating the seven days of creation for his special day
jokes.punchline: Mac replacing 'light' with 'Mac' in the biblical quote, making himself equivalent to God's first creation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Mac's narcissism and religious delusion combined perfectly. The pause before 'Mac' makes it even funnier. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: On the first day, God said, 'Let there be... Mac.'

jokes.text: Seven straight hours of lecturing? Yeah, and five hours alone dedicated to the evils of homosexuality... from him? Did anyone else notice that he had an erection the entire time?
jokes.setup: The group discussing Mac's long religious lecture
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Mac had an erection while lecturing about homosexuality being evil
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Mac's repressed homosexuality and self-denial. The casual way they discuss his obvious arousal is peak Always Sunny dark comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Seven straight hours of lecturing? Yeah, and five hours alone dedicated to the evils of homosexuality... from him?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Did anyone else notice that he had an erection the entire time?

jokes.text: There we have what? Uh, the what...? What are you saying? It's water. I'm saying 'water,' 'cause it said water, like, eight times. So, it's water. Water what? Water and badass, badass and water.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to connect a biblical passage to his 'badass' theme
jokes.punchline: His completely incoherent attempt to link water to being badass, Dennis's confusion, and Mac's nonsensical doubling down
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy showing Mac's inability to think on his feet. The repetitive 'water and badass, badass and water' is perfectly stupid.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There we have what? Uh, the what...? What are you saying?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's water. I'm saying 'water,' 'cause it said water, like, eight times. So, it's water.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Water what?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Water and badass, badass and water.

jokes.text: Holy shit, there's two of them.
jokes.setup: Country Mac arrives on his motorcycle to meet the group
jokes.punchline: Frank's horrified realization that there are now two Macs
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - Frank's dread at dealing with one Mac, now doubled. The simplicity makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Holy shit, there's two of them.

jokes.text: Well, I'm gonna jump from the Strawberry Mansion Bridge. / What?! / Holy shit! What do you mean, from it?
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his Project Badass video plan
jokes.punchline: Everyone's shock that Mac claims he'll actually jump from a bridge
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mac's fake badass persona. Sets up the reveal that he won't actually jump.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, I'm gonna jump from the Strawberry Mansion Bridge.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Holy shit! What do you mean, from it?

jokes.text: No, I'm not gonna jump. Of course I wouldn't jump. I draw it in later because it's like a post effect.
jokes.setup: The group assumes Mac will actually jump from the bridge for his badass video
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact explanation that he'll fake it with 'post effects'
jokes.explanation: Mac's cowardice masked as technical sophistication. His casual tone about the obvious fake-out is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, I'm not gonna jump. Of course I wouldn't jump. I draw it in later because it's like a post effect.

jokes.text: This is all sounding a little bit like a complaint from you guys. Who's complaining?! We don't know what's going on! We don't know you.
jokes.setup: The group is confused by Mac's contradictory video instructions
jokes.punchline: Mac threatening them with the complaint rule while Dennis explodes in frustration
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the complaint rule, with Dennis's mounting frustration being the real comedy here.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: This is all sounding a little bit like a complaint from you guys. Who's complaining?!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We don't know what's going on! We don't know you.

jokes.text: Evel Knievel didn't jump. Johnny Knoxville doesn't jump. Okay, this is big Hollywood stuff, okay?
jokes.setup: Mac trying to justify not actually jumping
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous comparison of his fake stunt to professional daredevils and Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Mac's delusion about his amateur video being 'Hollywood level' is in character but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Evel Knievel didn't jump. Johnny Knoxville doesn't jump. Okay, this is big Hollywood stuff, okay?

jokes.text: Country Mac actually jumps off the bridge and swims back up
jokes.setup: Mac is preparing his fake stunt while explaining his elaborate plan
jokes.punchline: Country Mac casually actually does the dangerous jump Mac won't do
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy - the ultimate contrast between Mac's fake badassery and Country Mac's effortless real badassery. Perfect structure and execution.

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: actually jumps off the bridge and swims back up

jokes.text: Did someone just go plop? / Looked like it. Someone just jumped.
jokes.setup: Mac's elaborate fake jumping setup
jokes.punchline: Country Mac actually jumped while no one was paying attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - while Mac stages elaborate fake badassery, Country Mac casually does the real thing. Great structure.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Did someone just go plop?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Looked like it. Someone just jumped.

jokes.text: No, that's not really badass, guys. That's dangerous. Um, can we put him on our shoulders and parade him around like Rudy? I'm feeling that now. No, no, no, no, I don't think that's gonna work anymore.
jokes.setup: Country Mac just performed an actually badass stunt
jokes.punchline: Mac dismissing genuine badassery as 'dangerous' while trying to control the narrative and schedule
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - his jealousy and need to control everything, even dismissing actual badassery. The schedule obsession is spot-on.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, that's not really badass, guys. That's dangerous. Um, can we put him on our shoulders and parade him around like Rudy? I'm feeling that now. No, no, no, no, I don't think that's gonna work anymore.

jokes.text: You know, kind of a bullshit day, so we're gonna move on to day four
jokes.setup: Mac explaining why he's skipping day three of creation
jokes.punchline: Dismissing God's creation of trees and nature as 'bullshit'
jokes.explanation: Mac casually dismissing part of divine creation as 'bullshit' is both absurd and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You know, kind of a bullshit day, so we're gonna move on to day four

jokes.text: Okay, I'm gonna need you guys to go ahead and ignore everything that this clown is saying. God created the stars 5,000 years ago.
jokes.setup: They're in a planetarium with a science presenter explaining astronomy
jokes.punchline: Mac interrupting with young-earth creationism and calling the scientist a 'clown'
jokes.explanation: Mac's anti-science stance and arrogance are in character. The 'clown' dismissal adds some edge but it's fairly predictable religious fundamentalist humor.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm gonna need you guys to go ahead and ignore everything that this clown is saying. God created the stars 5,000 years ago.

jokes.text: But there's herb. / Hmm? / Who wants to smoke a 'J'?
jokes.setup: Mac saying there's no herbs in the planetarium
jokes.punchline: Country Mac's pun on 'herb' and casual offer to smoke weed
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that establishes Country Mac as the cool, rebellious alternative to regular Mac.

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: But there's herb.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hmm?

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: Who wants to smoke a 'J'?

jokes.text: Who wants to smoke a 'J'? Oh, shit. You brought weed? No, no, no, we're not smoking weed, okay? It's still illegal in the state of Pennsylvania. Puff, puff, pass, bitches.
jokes.setup: Country Mac offers weed while Mac lectures about following God
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate switch from moral authority to rule-follower, while everyone else ignores him
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Mac's rigid rule-following and everyone else's casual law-breaking. Country Mac's casual cool vs Mac's uptightness.

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: Who wants to smoke a 'J'?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, shit. You brought weed? No, no, no, we're not smoking weed, okay? It's still illegal in the state of Pennsylvania.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Puff, puff, pass, bitches.

jokes.text: No, no, no, we're not smoking weed, okay? It's still illegal in the state of Pennsylvania.
jokes.setup: Country Mac offering weed to everyone
jokes.punchline: Mac's prissy, rule-following response when trying to be 'badass'
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Mac's fake badassery and his actual rule-following nature. Character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, no, no, we're not smoking weed, okay? It's still illegal in the state of Pennsylvania.

jokes.text: There's nothing badass about breaking the law, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining why they shouldn't smoke weed
jokes.punchline: Mac claiming breaking the law isn't badass, despite his entire 'badass' persona
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac contradiction - his version of 'badass' is completely sanitized and rule-following.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: There's nothing badass about breaking the law, Charlie.

jokes.text: Because if you don't, God will make a flood or a famine, okay? He will destroy you with tornadoes and earthquakes and AIDS. Where do you think AIDS came from? Uh, hello, God's gift to the gays.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to convert the group through fear
jokes.punchline: His casual, matter-of-fact delivery of AIDS as 'God's gift to the gays'
jokes.explanation: Dark but in-character homophobia from Mac. The casual 'hello' delivery adds some craft but it's fairly cheap shock humor.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Because if you don't, God will make a flood or a famine, okay? He will destroy you with tornadoes and earthquakes and AIDS. Where do you think AIDS came from? Uh, hello, God's gift to the gays.

jokes.text: But what about sunsets? Oh... French kisses. Oh... The smell of rain. Yeah. That's why I believe in God. Yeah. Not because of the things that he takes away from us but because of the things that he gives to us.
jokes.setup: Mac's harsh fire-and-brimstone approach to religion
jokes.punchline: Country Mac's gentle, romantic alternative view of God through beautiful experiences
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Country Mac's zen-like positivity vs Mac's negativity. The romantic listing is genuinely touching and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: But what about sunsets? Oh... French kisses. Oh... The smell of rain. Yeah. That's why I believe in God. Yeah. Not because of the things that he takes away from us but because of the things that he gives to us.

jokes.text: Shut up, science bitch. Yeah. Yeah, shut up, science bitch!
jokes.setup: The planetarium scientist continues his presentation
jokes.punchline: Charlie's dismissive 'science bitch' insult being enthusiastically adopted by the group
jokes.explanation: Iconic Always Sunny moment. Charlie's anti-intellectualism delivered with perfect economy. Became a widely quoted meme - that's impact.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Shut up, science bitch.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, shut up, science bitch!

jokes.text: All these years I've been feeling like I hate karate. And, like, I hate Project Badass and, like... I hate God. Yes. But, like... I realize... you know what I really hate... is Mac.
jokes.setup: Dennis having a stoned revelation about his feelings
jokes.punchline: The slow build from hating abstract concepts to the personal revelation that he hates Mac specifically
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure - building from general concepts to the specific personal hatred. Dennis's stoned delivery makes it even funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: All these years I've been feeling like I hate karate. And, like, I hate Project Badass and, like... I hate God. Yes. But, like... I realize... you know what I really hate... is Mac.

jokes.text: Like, he's made all those cool things suck. Not only is he, like, ruining my life... but with all this God shit that he's into... he could be ruining my afterlife.
jokes.setup: Dennis continuing his stoned Mac hatred rant
jokes.punchline: The escalation from ruining his current life to potentially ruining his eternal afterlife
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from the previous joke. The cosmic scale of Mac's negative influence is absurd and perfectly Dennis.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Like, he's made all those cool things suck. Not only is he, like, ruining my life... but with all this God shit that he's into... he could be ruining my afterlife.

jokes.text: I thought that one was gonna... Let me try.
jokes.setup: The group is high and apparently trying to break boards or do karate moves
jokes.punchline: Dee failing at whatever karate move she attempted
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy moment. Gets a mild laugh but nothing special - fairly predictable failure.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I thought that one was gonna... Let me try.

jokes.text: Okay, everybody grab some grease. Why? Because we're gonna grease up these beefcakes. No! No! Come on! No!
jokes.setup: Mac has brought them to what appears to be a bodybuilding event
jokes.punchline: The horrified reaction to being asked to grease up muscular men
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe setup - Mac's obliviousness to how inappropriate this is, contrasted with the group's horror.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Okay, everybody grab some grease. Why? Because we're gonna grease up these beefcakes.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No! No! Come on! No!

jokes.text: Chicks? No. Dudes. I'm into dudes. Oh... you're gay. Loud and proud, brother. Loud and proud.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking if there are women at the bodybuilding event
jokes.punchline: Country Mac's casual, confident coming out contrasted with Mac's repression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - Country Mac's confidence about his sexuality directly contrasts Mac's denial. The casual 'loud and proud' delivery is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Chicks?

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: No. Dudes. I'm into dudes.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh... you're gay.

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: Loud and proud, brother. Loud and proud.

jokes.text: It's so much more comfortable when someone's gay and open about it. And, like, I know we've never said this as a group, but... Mac's gay. Yeah, Mac's gay. He's gay. He's gay.
jokes.setup: Country Mac's casual openness about being gay
jokes.punchline: The group finally openly acknowledging Mac's obvious closeted homosexuality
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to years of Mac's denial. The matter-of-fact way they all agree, contrasted with his continued obliviousness, is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's so much more comfortable when someone's gay and open about it.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: And, like, I know we've never said this as a group, but... Mac's gay.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, Mac's gay.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He's gay.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: He's gay.

jokes.text: He's got the boner again. Yeah. Yeah. Well, at least this time it makes sense, huh?
jokes.setup: Mac is around the muscular men at the bodybuilding event
jokes.punchline: Callback to his earlier erection during the anti-gay lecture, but now it 'makes sense' given the context
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that adds new meaning to the earlier moment. The casual observation makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: He's got the boner again.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, at least this time it makes sense, huh?

jokes.text: Um, not technically. Um, only because I haven't subscribed to one... discipline. But you are good enough to fight in a black belt tournament. 'Cause you're wearing a black belt. Of course.
jokes.setup: Mac wearing a black belt but being questioned about his actual skill level
jokes.punchline: Mac's convoluted excuse about not 'subscribing to one discipline' and Dennis's sarcastic logic
jokes.explanation: Mac's pseudo-intellectual excuse for his fake expertise is perfectly in character. Dennis's deadpan response adds to it.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Um, not technically. Um, only because I haven't subscribed to one... discipline.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: But you are good enough to fight in a black belt tournament. 'Cause you're wearing a black belt. Of course.

jokes.text: Block, point, Mac! Shit. Guys, did you see that? I got a point! I got a point!
jokes.setup: Mac is getting destroyed in the karate tournament
jokes.punchline: His desperate celebration of scoring a single point while clearly losing badly
jokes.explanation: Mac's delusional focus on his tiny success while ignoring his overall failure is perfectly in character.

dialogue.line: Block, point, Mac!
Shit.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Guys, did you see that? I got a point! I got a point!

jokes.text: Country Mac beats up Mac's opponent with a beer bottle while shirtless
jokes.setup: Mac is losing his karate match badly
jokes.punchline: Country Mac casually wins the fight using a beer bottle and no protective gear
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy - the ultimate contrast between Mac's formal martial arts failure and Country Mac's effortless street fighting success.

dialogue.line: Country Mac beats up Mac's opponent with a beer bottle while shirtless

jokes.text: I scored an actual point in an actual karate tournament and against an actual black belt. Country Mac beat the shit out of that guy with a beer and his T-shirt.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to claim victory after his terrible performance
jokes.punchline: The contrast between Mac's technical 'achievement' and Country Mac's obviously superior real-world effectiveness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac denial - clinging to technicalities while ignoring obvious reality. Dennis's matter-of-fact correction adds to the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I scored an actual point in an actual karate tournament and against an actual black belt.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Country Mac beat the shit out of that guy with a beer and his T-shirt.

jokes.text: You bring a knife to a fight, you better be ready to use it, you jabroni. Just... don't come back here, you punks.
jokes.setup: A threatening stranger approaches the group
jokes.punchline: Country Mac's effortless intimidation and casual dismissal of the threat
jokes.explanation: Country Mac's actual badassery contrasted with Mac's fake version. 'Jabroni' is a nice touch that adds personality.

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: You bring a knife to a fight, you better be ready to use it, you jabroni. Just... don't come back here, you punks.

jokes.text: I gave him an ocular patdown. Assessed the threat level. Clocked a knife in his boot.
jokes.setup: Country Mac somehow knew the stranger had a weapon
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact explanation of his 'ocular patdown' technique
jokes.explanation: Iconic Always Sunny moment. 'Ocular patdown' became a widely quoted phrase. Perfect blend of absurd and badass that only Country Mac could deliver.

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: I gave him an ocular patdown. Assessed the threat level. Clocked a knife in his boot.

jokes.text: Guys, I may have had a little bit of an accident in my pants, so I think I got to change out of this gi.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to maintain dignity after the confrontation
jokes.punchline: His admission that he soiled himself during the scary encounter
jokes.explanation: Mac's cowardice and embarrassment contrasted with Country Mac's heroics. The euphemistic 'accident' adds some craft to basic scatological humor.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Guys, I may have had a little bit of an accident in my pants, so I think I got to change out of this gi.

jokes.text: You made a poopy in your pants! Did anybody else do a poopoo in their pants? No. Of course not. This is our head of security!
jokes.setup: Mac's embarrassing admission about soiling himself
jokes.punchline: Charlie's childish mockery and Frank's incredulous reaction about their 'head of security'
jokes.explanation: Charlie's childish language is in character. Frank's incredulous reaction adds to Mac's humiliation but it's fairly basic humor.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You made a poopy in your pants! Did anybody else do a poopoo in their pants? No. Of course not. This is our head of security!

jokes.text: Country Mac for Paddy's Mac. We send Mac to the country, bring Country Mac here. Yeah, to the bar. That's not a bad idea.
jokes.setup: The group is frustrated with Mac's incompetence and lies
jokes.punchline: Their casual discussion of replacing Mac with his superior cousin like trading players
jokes.explanation: The casual cruelty of discussing Mac like a trade commodity while he's nearby is perfectly in character for the group.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Country Mac for Paddy's Mac.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: We send Mac to the country, bring Country Mac here.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah, to the bar.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's not a bad idea.

jokes.text: It's not my style. I just go with the flow. Let the wind blow through my hair.
jokes.setup: The group expects a speech from Country Mac about joining them
jokes.punchline: His zen-like, effortless acceptance contrasted with Mac's neediness and over-explaining
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Country Mac's effortless cool vs Mac's desperate need for validation and control. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Country Mac
dialogue.line: It's not my style. I just go with the flow. Let the wind blow through my hair.

jokes.text: Country Mac crashes his motorcycle and dies
jokes.setup: Country Mac rides away on his motorcycle, having impressed everyone
jokes.punchline: His immediate, sudden death from a motorcycle crash
jokes.explanation: Shocking, dark comedy twist. The suddenness and matter-of-fact way it happens is perfectly Always Sunny. Changes the entire dynamic instantly.

dialogue.line: Country Mac crashes his motorcycle and dies

jokes.text: He wasn't the kind of guy that could score a point in a black belt karate contest. And it turns out, he was totally queer. Which, as we all know, is a sin.
jokes.setup: Mac delivering a eulogy for Country Mac
jokes.punchline: Mac's inability to acknowledge Country Mac's superiority, plus his homophobic response to his own cousin's death
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Mac moment - even in death, he can't acknowledge Country Mac's superiority and turns to homophobia. The irony is thick and dark.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He wasn't the kind of guy that could score a point in a black belt karate contest. And it turns out, he was totally queer. Which, as we all know, is a sin.

jokes.text: So I will ask for a moment of silence, in which I will beg God's forgiveness for Country Mac's evil, homo ways, while simultaneously doing a series of karate moves that I know Country Mac would've enjoyed so much.
jokes.setup: Mac's already inappropriate eulogy continues
jokes.punchline: His plan to combine prayer with karate moves as a tribute to his dead gay cousin
jokes.explanation: Peak Mac delusion - turning his cousin's death into an opportunity for his karate performance while being homophobic. The combination is brilliantly awful.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So I will ask for a moment of silence, in which I will beg God's forgiveness for Country Mac's evil, homo ways, while simultaneously doing a series of karate moves that I know Country Mac would've enjoyed so much.

jokes.text: Well, guys, I think the real lesson here is that there's nothing badass about riding a motorcycle without a helmet. Right. Plus, he was drunk all the time. Yeah. You know what's badass? Being alive.
jokes.setup: Country Mac just died doing the exact things Mac claims to admire
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate pivot to safety lectures and claiming that being alive is the real badassery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - his complete inability to learn the right lessons. Turning Country Mac's death into validation of his cowardly approach is brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, guys, I think the real lesson here is that there's nothing badass about riding a motorcycle without a helmet. Right. Plus, he was drunk all the time. Yeah. You know what's badass? Being alive.

jokes.text: Actually, we won't. Because it's officially Frank Day, so... All right, Pee Country Mac.
jokes.setup: Mac planning to honor Country Mac by taking his ashes to the countryside
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately cutting off the tribute because his day has started, followed by his crude dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - the group's selfishness immediately trumps any genuine emotion. Frank's crude 'Pee Country Mac' is the perfect final insult to his memory.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Actually, we won't. Because it's officially Frank Day, so...

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: All right, Pee Country Mac.
